stories.title: FY2027 Budget Public Input Period Open Through April 24 — How to Weigh In
stories.excerpt: Durham County is accepting public comments on the proposed FY2027 budget through April 24. Residents can submit written comments online or sign up to speak at the April 24 public hearing at 7 PM in Commissioners Chambers.

stories.title: BOCC Approves Affordable Housing Bond Drawdown at April 14 Meeting
stories.excerpt: Commissioners voted 4–1 to approve the latest tranche of the $95M affordable housing bond, directing $12M toward new workforce housing construction in East and South Durham.

stories.title: Durham County Seeks Applicants for Housing Advisory Board — Deadline May 1
stories.excerpt: Durham County is seeking residents to serve on the Housing Advisory Board, which advises commissioners on affordable housing policy. Applications are due May 1 at dconc.gov.

stories.title: DPS Board Approves FY2027 Budget Request, Sends Record Ask to Commissioners
stories.excerpt: The Durham Public Schools Board of Education officially submitted a $412M budget request to county commissioners — the largest in district history — prioritizing teacher pay raises, mental health counselors, and deferred capital maintenance.

stories.title: County Manager to Present FY2027 Recommended Budget May 19
stories.excerpt: Durham County Manager Kimberly Sowell will present the recommended FY2027 budget to the Board of Commissioners on May 19 at 9 AM. The full budget document will be made available online at dconc.gov.

stories.title: Planning Commission Reviews Rezoning Proposals for South Durham Mixed-Use Corridor
stories.excerpt: The City-County Planning Commission will hear three rezoning applications at its April 23 meeting, including a major mixed-use development near the I-40 and NC-55 interchange that would add 600+ housing units.

stories.title: Durham County Crisis Intervention Co-Responder Program Expanding to All Shifts
stories.excerpt: Following a successful two-year pilot, the county's mental health co-responder program — which pairs licensed clinicians with law enforcement — will expand to full 24/7 coverage by July 2026.

stories.title: GoTriangle Secures $28M Federal Grant for Durham Bus Rapid Transit Study
stories.excerpt: The Federal Transit Administration has awarded GoTriangle a $28 million grant to advance environmental review and design for a bus rapid transit corridor connecting downtown Durham, Duke University, and RTP.

stories.title: Durham County Library Wins State Award for Digital Equity Programs
stories.excerpt: The Durham County Library system received the NC Library Association's Community Impact Award for its digital navigator program, which has helped over 2,400 residents access online government services since 2024.

stories.title: Ellerbe Creek Watershed Restoration Breaks Ground in Northeast Durham
stories.excerpt: Durham County Stormwater Services and NC DEQ officially broke ground on the $2M Ellerbe Creek restoration project, which will stabilize 1.4 miles of degraded streambank and improve water quality for downstream communities.
